description: Summary of LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA observing runs from O1 through O4b. Dates and GPS times verified against GWOSC data release pages.

observing_runs.notes: First direct detection of gravitational waves (GW150914, September 14 2015). Only LIGO Hanford and LIGO Livingston operational. Virgo was offline undergoing Advanced Virgo upgrades.

Advanced Virgo (V1) joined on August 1 2017, forming the first three-detector network. GW170817 was the first binary neutron star merger detected, accompanied by electromagnetic counterparts. GW170814 was the first event detected by all three detectors simultaneously.

Significantly improved sensitivity over O2. GW190412 was the first asymmetric-mass binary black hole detection. GW190425 was the second binary neutron star candidate. GW190814 had a secondary component in the mass gap between neutron stars and black holes. O3 was paused after O3a for commissioning.
O3b ended early on March 27 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. GW200105 and GW200115 were the first confident neutron star-black hole merger candidates.

Virgo was not operational during O4a due to commissioning activities. KAGRA participated with limited sensitivity. GW230529 was a neutron star-black hole merger candidate. Catalog GWTC-4.0 covers O4a detections.

Virgo rejoined the network for O4b. Data publicly released via GWOSC. Catalog GWTC-5.0 covers O4b detections. A small amount of additional data from April 6-10 2024 (ER16) is included in the data release starting at GPS 1396417050.
